title: One Beach Toy Sold 200 Million Times — And Made Him Rich #Shorts
desc: In the late 1930s, Fred Morrison was tossing a cake pan on a California beach when a stranger paid a quarter for a pan that cost him a nickel — and he saw a business. He spent years turning it into a plastic flying disc, and in 1957 sold the rights to Wham-O for a cut of every one sold. The world renamed it the Frisbee, over 200 million sold, and the royalties made him a millionaire — he did the work once and got paid for decades.
